title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S11
body:
**Scene 11: 13:00–14:30 · Walter Walks Through the City**

HERO FRAME: Wide aerial of an empty American downtown, 1949 · taxis stopped at intersections, doors open · pigeons on streetlamps · a small figure (Walter, from behind, hat and overcoat) walking down the center of the empty avenue

MOTION: kling — the lone figure walks slowly forward. Pigeons take off from a streetlamp in a small flutter. A traffic light cycles red-yellow-green-red. A piece of paper blows past at street level. The wind moves a flag at half-mast.

MOOD: Twilight Zone *Time Enough at Last* energy.

AUDIO: Walter: *"I walk most days. I walk to keep moving. I walk to listen. I walk because if there is anyone else, I want to be findable."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
